skill.name: git-pushing
skill.description: Stage, commit, and push git changes with conventional commit messages. Use when user wants to commit and push changes, mentions pushing to remote, or asks to save and push their work.
skill.summary: Automates git workflow with staging, conventional commits, and push with upstream tracking.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The shell script uses standard git commands with hardcoded arguments. Documentation examples in SKILL.md are markdown code blocks, not executable code. No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Automate Git Commit and Push Workflow
content.value_statement: Streamline your git workflow by automating staging, conventional commits, and pushing with upstream tracking. Eliminate repetitive git commands with a single skill invocation.
actual_capabilities: Stage all changed files automatically with git add
Push changes to remote repository with upstream tracking
Support custom commit messages via parameter
limitations: Requires git repository to be initialized
Requires remote origin to be configured for push operations
Does not handle merge conflicts automatically
Requires authentication credentials to be pre-configured

use_cases.title: Quick Save and Push
use_cases.description: Save work-in-progress changes to remote repository with a single command
use_cases.target_user: Developers who frequently backup work to remote

use_cases.title: Feature Completion Workflow
use_cases.description: Commit and push completed features with proper conventional commit format
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers following conventional commit standards

use_cases.title: Automated Git Operations
use_cases.description: Integrate git operations into AI-assisted development workflows
use_cases.target_user: Teams using AI coding assistants for development

prompt_templates.title: Basic Push
prompt_templates.prompt: Push my current changes to the remote repository
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to quickly save and push work

prompt_templates.title: Push with Custom Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Commit and push with message: feat: add user authentication
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to specify a conventional commit message

prompt_templates.title: Save Work to GitHub
prompt_templates.prompt: Save these changes and push them to GitHub
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to backup work to remote

prompt_templates.title: Complete Feature Push
prompt_templates.prompt: I finished the login feature. Commit with proper message and push to remote
prompt_templates.scenario: User completed a feature and wants to share it

output_examples.output: Successfully staged 3 files, created commit 'chore: update code', and pushed to branch 'main' with upstream tracking set

output_examples.input: Commit and push: feat: add search functionality
output_examples.output: Successfully committed with message 'feat: add search functionality' and pushed to branch 'feature/search' with upstream tracking

best_practices: Review staged changes before pushing to ensure no sensitive files are included
Use meaningful conventional commit messages that describe the change purpose
Verify remote repository access and authentication before initiating push operations
anti_patterns: Do not push commits containing hardcoded credentials or API keys
Avoid pushing large binary files or build artifacts to the repository
Do not use this skill when working with sensitive or classified code repositories

faq.question: What happens if I have untracked files?
faq.answer: The skill stages all changes including untracked files using git add. Review your changes before running to ensure nothing unintended is included.

faq.question: Can I specify a custom branch to push to?
faq.answer: The skill pushes to the current branch. Change branches with git checkout before using this skill if you need to push to a different branch.

faq.question: What if I don't have a remote configured?
faq.answer: The push operation will fail. You need to add a remote with git remote add origin URL before using this skill.

faq.question: Does this skill handle merge conflicts?
faq.answer: No, merge conflicts must be resolved manually before using this skill. The git commit will fail if there are unresolved conflicts.

faq.question: What commit message format is used by default?
faq.answer: The default message is 'chore: update code' following conventional commit format. You can provide a custom message as a parameter.

faq.question: Does this work with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill uses standard git commands that work with any git remote provider including GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and self-hosted git servers.
